message:
fix: RAII pool guards, WebSocket write-permit rewrite, zombie reap, epoll TOCTOU, semaphore baton, compression content-length

Database pool (src/database/pool.rs):
- Introduce ValidationGuard and CreationGuard RAII types to guarantee the pool's
  total-connection counter is decremented on any exit path (error, panic, early
  return) during checkout validation and new-connection creation. Previously, a
  panic between incrementing `inner.total` and the error-handling decrement would
  permanently leak a slot, eventually starving the pool.

ReadToString (src/io/ext/read_ext.rs):
- Restore the `start_len` field and buffer-truncation-on-error behavior that was
  removed in a prior commit. Without rollback, a failed read_to_string leaves
  partially-decoded UTF-8 fragments appended to the caller's buffer, violating
  the contract that the buffer is unchanged on error. All three error paths
  (read error, incomplete UTF-8 at EOF, invalid UTF-8 mid-stream) now truncate
  back to the original length.

WebSocket split (src/net/websocket/split.rs):
- Replace the poll_fn-based write-permit acquisition with a proper
  AcquireWritePermitFuture struct that tracks waiters by unique ID. The previous
  approach used Waker::will_wake for deduplication, which is unreliable across
  executor re-polls with fresh wakers. The new design:
  - Assigns each waiter a monotonic ID on first poll
  - Updates the waker in-place on re-poll (instead of duplicate registration)
  - Implements Drop to remove the waiter and pass the baton to the next in line
    if the future is cancelled while at the head of the queue
  - Returns SplitWritePermit directly from poll() instead of requiring a
    separate construction step after the poll_fn resolves

Process (src/process.rs):
- Add cleanup_child_after_spawn_setup_failure() that calls both kill() and wait()
  on a child process. On Unix, kill() alone leaves a zombie until the parent
  reaps it; the prior code only called kill() in the stdin/stdout/stderr
  from_std error paths, leaking zombies on spawn-setup failures.
- Add test verifying that waitpid returns ECHILD after cleanup (proving the
  child was reaped).

Epoll reactor (src/runtime/reactor/epoll.rs):
- Fix TOCTOU race in deregistration: the fd-validity check (fcntl F_GETFD) was
  performed before the epoll_ctl DELETE attempt, creating a window where the fd
  could be closed by another thread between the check and the syscall. Move the
  validity check into the EBADF error handler so it runs only after the delete
  fails, eliminating the race.

Semaphore (src/sync/semaphore.rs):
- Fix spurious wakeup cascade in baton-passing: when the front waiter is
  removed, only pass the baton (wake the next waiter) if permits are actually
  available. Previously, removing a waiter always woke the next one, causing
  a chain of spurious wake-poll-sleep cycles when the semaphore was exhausted.
- Update tests to use Semaphore::new(2) with acquire(2) to properly test the
  front-waiter-needs-more-permits-than-available scenario.

Compression middleware (src/web/middleware.rs):
- Remove the stale Content-Length header after compression rewrites the response
  body. Leaving the original Content-Length causes HTTP clients to truncate or
  reject the compressed response. Add test asserting Content-Length is absent
  after gzip compression.

GF2 trace (src/trace/gf2.rs):
- Correct column_pivot test assertions to match the actual algorithm behavior:
  column_pivot returns the highest set bit (not lowest), so the triangle
  boundary matrix columns 0,1,2 have pivots at rows 1,2,None respectively
  after reduction (not 0,1,ambiguous).

Session (src/web/session.rs):
- Remove unnecessary .clone() on session_data before wrapping in Arc<Mutex<>>;
  the value is consumed immediately and cloning serves no purpose.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
